Dictionary.Phrase: DB Import Plus - Device {0} Properties Version {1}
Log the result of a query from the database (debugging)

On the Database tab, select the DBMS type and set the connection parameters. If standard fields are not enough, edit the connection string directly. Examples of connection strings are available at https://www.connectionstrings.com.
The driver configuration is split into import commands and export commands. Each import command has its own SQL query, command number, command code, name, description, enabled flag, data layout mode, and tag list. Disabled commands are skipped by the driver.
Import data layouts.
1. Tags based on the list of requested table columns. Use this mode when one result row contains values of several tags and column names match tag names. The driver reads the first returned row.
Example: SELECT COLUMN_1 AS TAGNAME_1, COLUMN_2 AS TAGNAME_2, COLUMN_3 AS TAGNAME_3 FROM DATATABLE_NAME WHERE CONDITION
The tag Name in the command tag list must match the column alias, for example TAGNAME_1. The tag Code must match the Rapid SCADA device tag code.
2. Tags based on the list of requested table rows. Use this mode when each returned row contains one tag. The query must return TAGNAME and TAGVALUE columns. The TAGTIME or TAGDATETIME column is optional.
Example: SELECT TAGNAME, TAGVALUE, TAGTIME FROM DATATABLE_NAME WHERE CONDITION
TAGNAME is matched with the tag Name in the command tag list. TAGVALUE is converted according to the configured tag format. TAGTIME and TAGDATETIME are treated as a UTC timestamp after conversion by the driver.
Historical data and slices. If TAGTIME or TAGDATETIME is not returned, values are written as current device data. If TAGTIME or TAGDATETIME is returned, values are sent as historical data. The driver groups historical tags by timestamp: tags with the same timestamp are placed into one device slice. Tags with different timestamps are placed into separate slices. To avoid creating too many slices, return rows ordered by time and use the same timestamp for values that must be archived together. Timestamp precision is seconds.
Import command notes. For column-based mode, the optional common TAGTIME or TAGDATETIME column applies to all returned tag columns. For row-based mode, each row can have its own TAGTIME or TAGDATETIME value. If the timestamp is empty or cannot be parsed, the value is processed as current data.
Export commands. Export commands write values from Rapid SCADA to a database. Use @cmdVal in SQL for the command value and @cmdNum for the command number. String command length is configured in the export command properties. Do not create regular tags at automatically generated command tag addresses.
Export command examples: INSERT INTO DATATABLE_NAME (COLUMN_1, COLUMN_2, COLUMN_3) VALUES ('TAGNAME00001', @cmdVal, GETDATE());
UPDATE DATATABLE_NAME SET COLUMN_2 = @cmdVal WHERE COLUMN_1 = @cmdNum;
MS SQL. Microsoft.Data.SqlClient is used for MS SQL connections. For remote connections, additional options may be required: Persist Security Info=True;Trust Server Certificate=True;
MySQL. If SSL mode has to be specified, use one of the following values: None or Disabled, Preferred or Prefered, Required, VerifyCA, VerifyFull.
Old project conversion. Old DrvDbDataTransfer XML configuration files can be converted by the DrvDbDataTransfer.Converter console utility. Run it without arguments, drag XML files or a configuration directory into the console, and press Enter. The utility creates an output directory near the source files and writes converted project files there.
Format of Tags from the Database
Maximum number of characters in a string command
Tags based on the list of requested table columns
Tags based on the list of requested table rows
Format of Tags from the Database
Maximum number of characters in a string command
Tags based on the list of requested table columns
Tags based on the list of requested table rows
Maximum number of characters in word
